news.title: AI Chips Update - NVIDIA and Wowza Tackle Synthetic Video Threats Globally
news.summary: Wowza has announced the deployment of a groundbreaking synthetic video detection solution integrated with NVIDIA's Synthetic Video Detector (SVD). This integration provides real-time AI-generated video detection capabilities across more than 35,000 of Wowza's video deployments globally, utilizing existing NVIDIA infrastructure. news.title: Can Google and Tesla Keep the AI Trade Going?
news.summary: This is a big day for the market, with Google owner Alphabet and electric vehicle manufacturer Tesla both set to report after the closing bell to kick off earnings season for Big Tech. Alphabet's earnings could be a make-or-break moment for the artificial-intelligence trade, which has faltered in recent weeks amid concerns about the rise of cheaper Chinese chatbots.

news.title: The Long Bond Is Making People Nervous
news.summary: The Congressional Budget Office in January 2001 predicted such large future budget surpluses that it said all redeemable U.S. government debt could be paid off within five years.  Treasury reversed course five years later as deficits instead widened, yet those bonds remain an afterthought.  The long bond isn’t as closely followed as the 10-year note, the benchmark shown daily in this newsletter and important for things like mortgage rates.

news.title: Intel results to test if AI-fueled rally has room to run
news.summary: Intel's results on Thursday will show whether the American chip icon has the numbers to back a Wall Street rally that has ‌sent its shares nearly three times higher this year, as its turnaround ‌push wins over investors and the AI buildout powers demand.  Investors are also eager for details on potential new customers for its contract manufacturing business, which has shown growing traction with deals including a ‌Tesla tie-up.  • Among the biggest ⁠questions for Intel is a potential deal to manufacture processors for Apple that U.S. President Donald Trump announced in April but neither ⁠company has confirmed.
